other_observers: viewed with Brandon Percival and Mark Peterson; Larry Arnold first found this bird
specific_location: back yard of Larry and Missy Arnold on the west side of Grand Junction.
description: This was a stoinking male Larry's Goldfinch, identified by black crown and bib, contrasting strongly with a bright yellow breast and upper belly. The gray back in turn contrasted nicely with smart canary yellow edgings on lesser and greater wing coverts, as well as the yellow edging to the bird's primaires. Gray on flanks and nape as well as white undertail coverts. Very distinctive white pattern in the outer rectix as visible from my photo, alone distinguishes this as a Lawrence's Goldfinch, separable from both American and Lesser Goldfinch.Behaviors: When seen, first perched on top of Juniperus sp. and then landing on thistle feeder. Unfortunately the bird moves behind the thistle feeder where only its tail and undertail coverts are visible. Feeds for 45 seconds.Call: yes, bell-like tinking heard on two occassions.Plumage: adult alternate
